operating_state: Net sales decreased to $6.4 billion in Q2 2025 from $6.8 billion in Q1 2025, driven by lower commodity prices across oil, NGL, and natural gas, partially offset by higher crude oil and NGL sales volumes. Six-month sales increased to $13.2 billion from $12.8 billion in 2024, primarily due to higher oil volumes from a full six months of CrownRock production and higher domestic natural gas and NGL prices, offset by lower oil prices. Average daily sales volumes increased to 1,394 Mboe for the six months, up from 1,218 Mboe in 2024, mainly attributable to the CrownRock acquisition.
cost_structure: Depreciation, depletion and amortization increased to $3.9 billion for the six months from $3.5 billion in 2024, primarily due to full six months of CrownRock asset production. Oil and gas segment earnings decreased due to lower commodity prices partially offset by lower lease operating expenses. Chemical segment margins compressed from lower PVC pricing and higher ethylene costs, while midstream margins improved from transportation capacity optimization in the Permian Basin.
material_events: Occidental completed warrant exercises in April 2025, issuing 41.9 million shares for approximately $890 million in proceeds. The company repaid $2.3 billion in debt during the six months, including senior notes and term loan reductions. Asset divestitures generated $1.5 billion in proceeds from non-core properties. Subsequent to quarter-end, an additional $700 million of the term loan was repaid, reducing principal debt to $21.4 billion.
macro_exposures: WTI oil prices averaged $63.74 per barrel in Q2 2025, down from $71.42 in Q1 2025 and $80.56 in Q2 2024, with continued volatility expected from geopolitical risks and OPEC actions. President Trump's April 2025 tariff policy imposing baseline tariffs on most imports with higher reciprocal rates creates cost pressures on suppliers and potential impacts on OxyChem's import-export operations.
forward_outlook: Management maintains strategic priorities of preserving asset base integrity, delivering sustainable dividend growth, and prioritizing excess cash flow for deleveraging until principal debt falls below $15 billion. The company continues advancing low-carbon ventures and carbon management solutions while enhancing cash-generative operations.
uncertainty_profile: Occidental faces potential put obligations on Zero Coupons in October 2025 requiring approximately $381 million payment if exercised in whole. The company maintains $2.2 billion in debt maturities due in 2026 while remaining compliant with all financing covenants. Environmental remediation liabilities continue under federal and state laws with ongoing proceedings at various sites.
